concerns.text: The plan validates the 'Visual Evidence Dependency' claim by comparing accuracy in 'Full Multimodal' vs. 'Ablated' (text-only) conditions. However, the plan relies on an 'LLM Judge' (llm_judge.py) to determine 'is_correct' (data-model.md). If the LLM Judge is a text-only model (as implied by the CPU-only constraint and the nature of ablation), it cannot independently verify answers that require visual evidence. This creates a circular validation: the metric (accuracy) is determined by a judge that lacks the very modality (vision) being tested. The ground truth for image-dependent questions must be derived from the dataset's original human annotations or a vision-capable oracle, not a text-only proxy, or the ablation test is tautological (text-only judge cannot see the image, so it cannot validate the image's necessity).

The plan proposes 'Regression analysis of Accuracy vs. Context Length' to claim performance degradation. However, the plan also states (Decision Log) that it will use 'API-based evaluation' for large models. If the API provider (e.g., GPT-4) internally truncates or compresses context beyond a certain threshold (e.g., 128K) regardless of the input token count sent, the 'Context Length' variable is not independent of the model's internal state. The analysis would measure the API's truncation policy, not the model's memory capacity. The plan must explicitly verify that the API actually processes the full 256K context window as a distinct variable, or the regression is measuring an artifact of the API gateway, not the scientific claim.

The plan assumes running 'LLaVA-1.5-7B' on a 7GB CPU-only runner. A 7B parameter model in 16-bit precision requires ~14GB of RAM just for weights, exceeding the 7GB limit. Even with 4-bit quantization (which the plan explicitly forbids for CPU runners), the overhead of CPU inference and context window (256K tokens) will likely cause OOM. The plan's 'sampled subset' strategy (10-50 questions) does not solve the per-sample memory requirement for the context window. The methodology is physically impossible as stated; the plan must either allow quantization (contradicting the 'forbidden' note) or restrict the local model to a much smaller architecture (e.g., <2B parameters) to be scientifically valid on the target hardware.
